fb_text:
Every field-service app dies the same death.

Week one: everyone's excited. Week two: the techs stop opening it. Week three: you're paying $200 a month for an icon on phones nobody touches.

I run a commercial plant service company in Atlanta — 50+ corporate and commercial properties across the metro. I watched this happen with every tool I tried. My guys aren't desk workers. They're not going to stand in a parking lot and fill out a form.

So I stopped asking them to.

FieldNotes works where they already live: text messages and voice notes. After a stop, my tech talks for 10 seconds like he's texting me. The AI turns it into the service log, the client recap, the follow-up list. No app to learn. No forms. Nothing to remember.

Week two looks exactly like week one.

If every tool you've bought died in your crew's pockets, try the one that isn't an app — 60-second demo, no install:
fieldnotesapp.io/app/try.html
